notes: Goyal et al. 2014, JAMA Internal Medicine; 47 RCTs, 3515 participants; moderate evidence for anxiety (ES 0.38), depression (ES 0.30), pain (ES 0.33); gold-standard meta-analysis; effects comparable to antidepressants without toxicities
2024 meta-analysis of 43 RCTs of mindfulness apps; small but significant effects on depression (g=0.24) and anxiety (g=0.28); updated evidence for digital delivery
2023 dose-ranging RCT; effects of meditation duration and type on well-being; informs dose-response question
